translation.PP Introduction text: Welcome to ft_transcendance. We are committed to protecting your personal information and your right to privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and share your information when you use our web application, particularly concerning the uploading of medical data (HL7) and interaction with our AI services.
translation.PP Account Information text: When you register, we collect your email, first name, last name, and a hashed version of your password.
translation.PP Medical Data text: If you use our HL7 to FHIR engine, you may upload HL7 files. We process this data strictly for conversion and AI analysis (RAG). Note: This is an academic project. Do NOT upload real, identifiable patient data. Use dummy data only.
translation.PP Use 1: To provide, operate, and maintain our application and AI engine.
translation.PP Use 2: To convert your uploaded HL7 files into structured FHIR formats.
translation.PP Use 3: To allow you to query data using our locally hosted Large Language Models (LLM).
translation.PP Use 4: To manage your user roles (e.g., admin capabilities).
translation.PP Data Security text: We use administrative, technical, and physical security measures to help protect your personal information, including WAF (Web Application Firewall), HTTPS, and HashiCorp Vault for secrets management. However, no electronic transmission over the internet or information storage technology can be guaranteed to be 100% secure.
translation.PP GDPR text: Under applicable data protection laws, you have the right to request access to, correct, or delete your personal data. You may exercise these rights by contacting an administrator or using the data management features provided in the application when available.
translation.PP Updates text: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. The updated version will be indicated by an updated 'Revised' date and the updated version will be effective as soon as it is accessible.
translation.TOS Acceptance text: By accessing and using ft_transcendance, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you disagree with any part of these terms, you may not access the service.
translation.TOS Description text: ft_transcendance is an academic project developed as part of the 42 curriculum. It provides a web-based platform featuring an HL7 to FHIR conversion engine, local AI interactions (RAG and LLM), and advanced user permission management.
translation.TOS Conduct 1 text: This is a simulation/academic environment. You agree to strictly use mock or anonymized HL7 data. Under no circumstances should real Patient Health Information (PHI) be uploaded.
translation.TOS Conduct 2 text: You are responsible for safeguarding your password. You must not disclose your password to any third party.
translation.TOS Conduct 3 text: You agree not to use the application for any unlawful purpose or in any way that interrupts, damages, or impairs the service, including the WAF or backend microservices.
translation.TOS IP text: The software, code, and design of this application are the intellectual property of the developers under the context of the 42 curriculum. You may not copy, modify, or distribute our intellectual property without explicit permission.
translation.TOS Liability text: This service is provided 'AS IS' without warranties of any kind. The developers shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or any loss of profits or revenues, whether incurred directly or indirectly, or any loss of data.
translation.TOS Termination text: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account and access to the service at our sole discretion, without prior notice or liability, for any reason, including without limitation if you breach the Terms.
